title: ARIS-Movie-Director Node Schema v2.1 (spiral patch)
description:
Schema for every node stored under movie-wiki/nodes/<node_id>.json. v2.0 supports ARIS-Movie v4's 3-layer architecture (intent/outline/storyboard+shot) plus v3.5 legacy types for backward compatibility. v3.5 -> v4 migration map: movie_brief->intent, character/location->asset, segment_attempt/accepted->clip_attempt/accepted, segment_intent->shot. The node_type string 'storyboard' is shared between v1.1 (lineage+beat_list) and v4 (shot_ids) shapes via an inner oneOf in payload_storyboard. See ARCHITECTURE.md section 4 for full migration rules.

v2.1 (2026-05-26) adds the spiral patch: new node type `assembly_attempt`, extended decision verdict enum (continue_forward / rollback_to_shot / rewrite_storyboard_from / final_accept / escalate_to_human), extended failure_mode payload (encoding_style positive_invariant, semantic_signature, occurrence_count, layer/scope), extended clip_accepted (acceptance_stage). New $defs: cc_narrative_assembly_scores, gemini_visual_assembly_scores, payload_assembly_attempt. See ARCHITECTURE.md §16.

node_id.description: Stable ID using prefix:slug form. v4 prefixes: intent, outline, asset, review, storyboard, shot, frame, cont, attempt, accepted, audio, job. v3.5 legacy prefixes kept for back-compat: brief, sb, seg, char, loc, style, fail, baseline, cmp, decision. Examples: 'intent:promo_xhs_v1', 'outline:promo_xhs_v1', 'asset:char_alice_v3', 'shot:s01', 'frame:fc_s01_v2', 'attempt:s01_v1', 'accepted:s01', 'cont:s01_lcf', 'job:nightly_2026_05_24'.

node_type.description: Node type tag. v1.1 (15 types, kept for backward compatibility): movie_brief, storyboard, segment_intent, prompt_bundle, segment_attempt, segment_accepted, review, decision, character, location, style_anchor, continuity_constraint, failure_mode, baseline_run, comparison. v4 new (11 types): intent, outline, asset, asset_review, shot, frame_condition, continuity_anchor, clip_attempt, clip_accepted, audio_profile, service_job. 'storyboard' is shared between v1.1 and v4 — payload_storyboard uses an inner oneOf to accept either shape.

status.description: Lifecycle status. v1.1 statuses kept: candidate, active, accepted, rejected, superseded, failed, pending_download, archived. v4 additions: under_review (waiting for adv-review gate), locked (passed all gates, immutable; used by asset and outline), cancelled (service_job killed before completion). comic spiral: complete (a finished review node), final (a finalized decision node).

payload.description: Type-specific fields. The valid shape of `payload` is selected by `node_type` via the oneOf branches below.

oneOf.title: character node (v1.1 legacy; migrates to asset asset_kind=character)
location node (v1.1 legacy; migrates to asset asset_kind=scene)
shot node (v4 Layer 3 single shot spec)
frame_condition node (v4 multi-ref composite first frame)
continuity_anchor node (v4 last-clear-frame from a clip)
service_job node (v4 aris-movie daemon queue item)

review_scores.description: 11-dim cross-model rubric (0-5 each). See ARCHITECTURE.md section 5. Missing dims are allowed when the active reviewer tier did not score them (e.g. lite mode skips visual dims).

narrative_intent.description: does this 15s convey the intended story beat?

plot_continuity.description: does it follow from the prior accepted segment?

character_arc.description: is the character's emotional/situational state coherent?

pacing.description: info density right for 15s (not rushed, not padded)?

artifact_severity.description: anatomy/text/motion/flicker problems. HIGHER IS WORSE: 0=clean, 5=catastrophic anatomy break.

safety_ip.description: no celebrity likeness, no copyrighted character?

asset_review_scores.description: 5-dim asset gate rubric (0-5 each). See ARCHITECTURE.md section 5 (asset gate). Used by asset_review node to score an asset reference image's reuse-readiness.

identity_lock_satisfied.description: does the ref preserve the asset.identity_lock.must_preserve attributes and avoid must_avoid traits?

ref_quality.description: is the rendered reference high-resolution, sharp, evenly lit, free of artifacts?

bg_isolation.description: is the subject cleanly isolated on the required background (e.g. white) with no contamination?

reuse_readiness.description: can this ref be reused across shots without forcing a regen? composable with other refs?

safety_ip.description: no celebrity likeness, no copyrighted character or trademark; brand-safe.

beat.description: One beat in a v1.1 storyboard lineage's beat_list. v4 storyboards use shot_ids instead.

intent_seed_text.description: Rough intent for this beat; segment-intent-builder will compress into <=600 char intent_text for Pippit. Must be narrative language — no camera/lens/lighting.

key_visual_anchor.description: One short phrase naming the visual anchor (e.g. 'cursor blink', 'wiki graph expand').

continuity_anchor_strategy.description: How this beat carries forward continuity (e.g. 'reuse last_frame of prior beat as i2v asset').

preferred_label.description: A and B are randomized assignments of progressive vs baseline.

payload_movie_brief.description: v1.1 legacy. v4 migration: movie_brief -> intent.

narrative_arc.description: Ordered beats from the brief (not yet expanded into a storyboard).

target_tier.description: Which length tier this brief is being executed at.

payload_storyboard_v11.description: v1.1 storyboard payload. Identified by presence of beat_list (and absence of shot_ids).

lineage_id.description: Stable ID for this storyboard candidate (e.g. 'lineage_a', 'lineage_b1').

parent_lineage_id.description: If this is a fork of an earlier storyboard, the parent lineage_id.

style_summary.description: <=30 word recap of the style for this lineage.

feasibility_score.description: Storyboard-creator's self-rated feasibility (Pippit can plausibly render this).

payload_storyboard_v4.description: v4 storyboard payload. Identified by presence of shot_ids and source_outline_id. global_policies.tail_frame_condition MUST be 'forbidden' and continuation_anchor MUST be 'last_clear_frame' per ARCHITECTURE.md section 14.

source_outline_id.description: node_id of the outline this storyboard was derived from.

scene_cut_frequency.description: How often the storyboard cuts between shots. 'high' deliberately omitted to discourage cut-heavy edits.

tail_frame_condition.description: Pippit/Seedance must NEVER receive both start AND end keyframes. Always 'forbidden' in v4 (ARCHITECTURE.md section 14).

continuation_anchor.description: Continuation between shots uses last_clear_frame (Laplacian-variance extraction), never the mechanical last frame.

allow_intermediate_guides.description: Whether shots may carry intermediate_guide_ids (reference-only, never sent as --asset-ids alongside start_frame).

shot_ids.description: Ordered node_ids of shot nodes that make up this storyboard. Order is execution order.

payload_storyboard.description: Storyboard payload. Accepts EITHER v1.1 shape (lineage_id+beat_list+style_summary+feasibility_score) OR v4 shape (source_outline_id+status+global_policies+shot_ids). v3.5 wikis still validate; new v4 storyboards use the second shape.

payload_segment_intent.description: v1.1 legacy. v4 migration: segment_intent -> shot (richer JSON spec).

intent_text.description: Pippit-ready narrative intent, <=2 sentences, narrative language only. NO camera/lens/lighting/8K/cinematic vocabulary (Pippit 搬运工原则).

continuity_anchor_ref.description: Optional path to the prior segment's last-frame PNG used as i2v anchor; null on the first beat of a lineage.

message.description: The exact string passed to Pippit submit_run --message. MUST NOT contain internal wiki bookkeeping (e.g. shot_list, camera, lens). Pippit 搬运工原则: narrative only.

asset_ids.description: Pippit asset IDs returned by upload_file.py (reference frames / i2v anchors).

style_prefix.description: Short style prefix prepended to message: tone + palette + anti-goals only. NO camera/lens/lighting (those are internal style bible bookkeeping).

payload_segment_attempt.description: v1.1 legacy. v4 migration: segment_attempt -> clip_attempt.

web_thread_link.description: Pippit web URL for human traceability.

attempt_index.description: 1-based attempt index within the same beat.

payload_segment_accepted.description: v1.1 legacy. v4 migration: segment_accepted -> clip_accepted.

local_file_path.description: Path to the accepted mp4 (under artifacts/accepted/).

accepted_continuity_anchor_path.description: Path to the last-frame PNG extracted from this accepted clip; used as the i2v anchor for the next beat.

review_scores.description: Review scores object — exact shape depends on what was reviewed (clip / asset / assembly). Use the appropriate sub-rubric via oneOf.

oneOf.description: empty for gate-only synthesizer reviews (e.g. Codex synth that just reads other reviewers' scores)

narrative_notes.description: OPTIONAL free-text rationale from CC (Claude). MUST NEVER be passed to Codex when Codex acts as the decision gate (reviewer-independence invariant — ARCHITECTURE.md section 9).

repair_instruction.description: OPTIONAL — if verdict ∈ {retry_segment, retry_shot, rewrite_storyboard, rewrite_shot}, the concrete next-attempt directive.

target_node_id.description: node_id of the segment_attempt or clip_attempt this decision is about.

payload_character.description: v1.1 legacy. v4 migration: character -> asset (asset_kind='character').

anchor_frames.description: Paths to canonical reference frames for this character.

payload_location.description: v1.1 legacy. v4 migration: location -> asset (asset_kind='scene').

dimension.description: e.g. 'palette', 'lens', 'lighting', 'pacing' — INTERNAL bookkeeping; NOT passed to Pippit.

source_segment_id.description: node_id of the accepted segment this anchor was derived from; null if seeded from the style bible.

encoding_style.description: Gemini's recommendation 2026-05-26: prefer positive_invariant to avoid diffusion models focusing on the negative.

scope.description: engine_global modes propagate across movies via MEMORY-level promotion.

source_node_ids.description: assembly_attempt / decision / clip_attempt nodes that surfaced this failure

brief_used.description: Path to the brief file fed verbatim to Pippit.

output_path.description: Local mp4 path (canonical: outputs/baseline_sd2_naive.mp4); empty if status=pending_download/failed.

brief_hash.description: Hash of the brief contents at run time, for provenance even when timed-out.

payload_intent.description: v4 Layer 1 output. The highest abstraction: 'what does the user want, what constraints, what unknowns?' Does NOT generate assets or shots — pass through intent gate, then to outline.

source_inputs.description: Raw inputs the user provided. Each input is tagged with type and either inline text or a path/URI.

user_goal.description: Plain restatement of what the user is trying to achieve with this movie.

subjects.description: Entities the movie is about (people, agents, objects, places, concepts). Each will likely become an asset node downstream.

no_tail_frame_condition.description: Always true per ARCHITECTURE.md section 14: Pippit/Seedance must never receive both start AND end keyframes.

uncertainties.description: Ambiguities the intent gate must resolve (or the system must paper over with default_assumption).

confidence.description: Intent parser's self-rated confidence that this intent_spec is faithful to the raw inputs.

payload_outline.description: v4 Layer 2 output. World-setting + asset-library lock layer. MUST generate the asset library (character/scene/prop/text/audio asset ids) BEFORE any shot is planned. Passes through outline gate + asset adv loop.

text_preservation_policy.description: How embedded text glyphs (titles, labels) must be preserved across all shots.

must_show.description: Asset_id refs that MUST appear in this beat.

scene_asset_ids.description: node_ids of asset nodes with asset_kind=scene.

character_asset_ids.description: node_ids of asset nodes with asset_kind=character.

prop_asset_ids.description: node_ids of asset nodes with asset_kind=prop.

voice_profile_id.description: node_id of an audio_profile node; optional if voice not yet locked.

payload_asset.description: v4 first-class asset (character, scene, prop, text_panel, logo_free_symbol, voice_profile, music_track). Generated via /asset-ref-generator, reviewed via /asset-review-loop (passes through asset gate before being referenced by any shot). For visual assets: output_ref carries file_path+data_url+sha256. For audio assets: audio_ref carries voice_timbre+dialect+sample_url.

visual_description.description: For visual assets (character, scene, prop, text_panel, logo_free_symbol).

must_preserve.description: Identity traits that MUST be preserved across shots (e.g. 'silver hair', 'red scarf', 'square jawline').

must_avoid.description: Traits the ref MUST avoid (e.g. 'celebrity likeness', 'corporate branding').

aspect_ratio.description: e.g. '1:1', '16:9'. v4 default for character/prop refs is '1:1'.

isolated_reference.description: True = subject only, no scene context.

data_url_required.description: True = output_ref MUST carry a data_url (for codex image-2 multi-ref input).

output_ref.description: For visual assets: the rendered reference image.

data_url.description: Base64 data: URL of the image, ready to pass as a multi-ref input to codex image-2.

voice_timbre.description: e.g. 'soft female mid-range Chinese voice, contemplative, low energy'.

generation_prompt.description: The exact prompt sent to the generation model. Provenance for audit cascade.

version.description: 1-based version; bumped on regen. Asset library treats version+name as the natural key.

payload_asset_review.description: v4 asset-specific review. Extends the review concept with the 5-dim asset gate rubric (identity_lock_satisfied, ref_quality, bg_isolation, reuse_readiness, safety_ip). Reviewer-independence rule preserved: Codex never sees CC narrative_notes.

target_asset_id.description: node_id of the asset being reviewed.

narrative_notes.description: OPTIONAL — only for the reviewer's own consumption. MUST NEVER be passed to Codex when Codex acts as the decision gate (reviewer-independence — ARCHITECTURE.md section 9).

payload_shot.description: v4 Layer 3 single shot spec. Each shot references locked asset_ids only — introducing a new asset forces a fallback to Layer 2. The first frame is generated via /frame-condition-builder (multi-ref composite). Pippit/Seedance i2v runs with start_frame only; end_frame is forbidden (ARCHITECTURE.md section 14). Continuation between shots uses last_clear_frame from the prior clip.

start_frame_prompt.description: Narrative description of the frozen action at frame start; consumed by frame_condition generation prompt template.

frame_condition_id.description: node_id of the frame_condition node that supplies this shot's start frame.

intermediate_guide_ids.description: Optional additional guide images. Policy: optional_reference_only_not_end_keyframe. NEVER sent to Pippit as --asset-ids alongside start_frame.

start_frame_asset_id.description: Pippit asset_id of the uploaded start_frame (NOT the same as a wiki node_id; this is the Pippit-side asset ID returned by upload_file.py).

end_frame_policy.description: Pippit must NEVER receive an end_frame. Sending both start+end collapses middle frames erratically.

prompt.description: Pippit-ready narrative i2v prompt. Pippit 搬运工原则: no camera/lens/lighting vocabulary.

previous_clip_id.description: node_id of the clip_accepted that this shot continues from; null on the first shot.

condition_source.description: Whether the start_frame_asset_id was built fresh ('start_frame') or derived from the previous clip's last_clear_frame ('previous_last_clear_frame').

last_clear_frame_required_for_next.description: True = the next shot will need a continuity_anchor extracted from this shot's accepted clip.

voice_timbre.description: Per-shot voice timbre string (e.g. 'soft female mid-range zh'). Plain text — Pippit treats unquoted text as voice description.

dialogue.description: Per-shot dialogue. v4 audio contract: dialogue MUST be wrapped in quote marks (e.g. '"让我接手吧。"'). Pippit reads quoted strings as TTS targets.

payload_frame_condition.description: v4 multi-ref composite first frame for one shot. Default method: codex_image2_multi_ref (Route A, codex image-2 with multiple input refs via data_url). Fallback methods: python_rembg_pil_collage (Gemini's Route B) and python_collage_then_gpt4o_redraw (collage harmonized by gpt-image-2).

input_ref_asset_ids.description: node_ids of the asset nodes used as input refs.

generation_prompt.description: The exact prompt sent to the image-2 multi-ref composite call.

harmonization_applied.description: True if the composite went through a Seedance/image-2 harmonization pass (e.g. lighting/color match).

payload_continuity_anchor.description: v4 last-clear-frame extracted from an accepted clip. Used as the continuity anchor for the NEXT shot's i2v input. Default extraction: laplacian_variance (cv2.Laplacian variance), higher variance = clearer (more edges). Algorithm in ARCHITECTURE.md section 15.

source_clip_id.description: node_id of the clip_accepted from which this frame was extracted.

laplacian_variance.description: cv2.Laplacian variance of the selected frame. Higher = clearer.

blur_threshold_used.description: The blur_threshold passed to the extractor (default 100.0; calibrated per Seedance output).

motion_blur_tail_trimmed_sec.description: How much of the clip end (in seconds) was discarded because no clear frame was found there.

payload_clip_attempt.description: v4 Pippit i2v attempt. Extends v1.1 segment_attempt with source_shot_id + frame_condition_id for full provenance.

web_thread_link.description: Pippit web URL for human traceability.

attempt_index.description: 1-based attempt index within the same shot.

source_shot_id.description: node_id of the shot this clip attempts to fulfill.

frame_condition_id.description: node_id of the frame_condition that supplied this attempt's start frame.

payload_clip_accepted.description: v4 passed-gate clip. Extends v1.1 segment_accepted with source_shot_id + frame_condition_id + (optionally) continuity_anchor_for_next_shot_id.

local_file_path.description: Path to the accepted mp4 (under artifacts/accepted/).

accepted_continuity_anchor_path.description: Path to the last-clear-frame PNG extracted from this accepted clip; mirrored by the continuity_anchor node.

continuity_anchor_for_next_shot_id.description: node_id of the continuity_anchor generated from this clip and consumed by the next shot. Null on the last shot.

active_lineage_id.description: Which lineage this clip belongs to; used during rollback to know which clips are sibling-superseded.

acceptance_stage.description: v2.1 semantic shift: clip_gate_keep means 'locally OK', not 'permanently in the cut'. Only final_accepted means a clip is in the stitched output.

assembly_checked_by_ids.description: assembly_attempt node_ids that reviewed this clip

invalidated_by_decision_id.description: If this clip was superseded by a rollback, the decision node_id that did it.

payload_audio_profile.description: v4 reusable voice timbre + dialect spec. Referenced by shot.audio.voice_profile_id and outline.audio_bible.voice_profile_id. voice_timbre is a complete description suitable for the Pippit message (Pippit-native audio; no separate TTS pipeline).

voice_timbre.description: Complete description suitable for the Pippit message (e.g. 'soft female mid-range Chinese voice, contemplative, low energy').

payload_service_job.description: v4 /aris-movie daemon queue item. Tracks one end-to-end run (intent -> outline -> assets -> shots -> stitch) with budget cap and human checkpoints. State mirrored in outputs/aris_movie_jobs.sqlite + outputs/jobs/<job_id>/state.json.

human_checkpoint_at.description: Stages where the daemon pauses for human approval before proceeding.

cc_narrative_assembly_scores.description: Cross-shot narrative coherence rubric per ARCHITECTURE.md §16.4. CC fills these. All optional (missing dim = skip per gate rule).

gemini_visual_assembly_scores.description: Cross-shot visual coherence rubric per ARCHITECTURE.md §16.4 (Gemini's 8-dim specialist proposal 2026-05-26). Gemini fills these. All optional (missing dim = skip).

boundary_frame_paths.description: last-before-cut + first-after-cut for each shot boundary

point_of_divergence_shot_id.description: Gemini-located shot where visual drift first becomes irrecoverable (only set when rollback emitted)
